channel.title: AI Firehose: What Is New
channel.description: We read the whole AI firehose so you do not have to, then distill it into one brief: what is new, what is breaking out, and what is worth your time, across the day, week, month, and quarter. You take what you want and leave the rest.

item.title: RIP Claude Fable, open-source AI unleashed, full body avatars, new Google models, new TTS: AI NEWS
item.description: AI Search: AI Search presents a news roundup covering GLM 5.2, Kimi K2.7, MiniMax M3, and Claude Fable along with new tools for avatars, translation, and text-to-speech. The video reviews emerging models and techniques across vision, language, and diffusion domains. Multiple open-source and closed systems are discussed including Gemini models and various research projects.

item.title: Is This The FASTEST AI Model In The World?!! (Xiaomi MiMo V2.5 Pro UltraSpeed)
item.description: Better Stack: Xiaomi and TileRT released MiMo-V2.5-Pro-UltraSpeed, a 1-trillion-parameter Mixture-of-Experts model achieving over 1,000 tokens per second on standard hardware. The model uses selective FP4 quantization, DFlash speculative decoding, and persistent GPU kernel runtime to optimize inference speed.

item.title: Fable 5 is never coming back, here's why
item.description: David Ondrej: David Ondrej discusses the banning of Fable 5, framing it as evidence of emerging social stratification. The video appears to argue that such restrictions signal the beginning of a permanent underclass divide.

item.title: This Is Bad... They Just Shut Down FABLE 5
item.description: AI Revolution: Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models were shut down after three days following US government order. The video argues this signals a broader pattern where frontier AI models may face government restrictions, raising questions about public access to powerful AI systems and government control over frontier AI deployment.

item.title: GPT-6 Is Launching Into a World OpenAI No Longer Controls
item.description: AI Master: The video argues that OpenAI's market dominance is eroding as competitors launch alternative models and integrations. Microsoft, Apple, and Google have reduced dependence on OpenAI by promoting their own LLMs through integrations like Project Polaris, Apple Intelligence with Gemini, and benchmark advances in Claude, while OpenAI deliberated between GPT-5.5 and GPT-6.

item.title: Claude Fable 5 is Now BANNED?!
item.description: Cole Medin: Cole Medin discusses the reported shutdown of Claude Fable 5 by US government order three days after launch, attributed to export-control directives and claimed jailbreak vulnerability. The video covers why the model was pulled, implications for developers, and benchmark comparisons against Opus 4.8.

item.title: 1,000+ Tokens/Sec: Google Just Shattered the AI Speed Limit (DiffusionGemma)
item.description: Better Stack: Google DeepMind released DiffusionGemma, a model that applies diffusion techniques from image generation to text via uniform state diffusion over multiple bidirectional passes. The architecture shifts local LLMs from memory-bound to compute-bound, achieving generation speeds exceeding 1,000 tokens per second on H100 GPUs. The trade-off is speed versus maximum output quality.

item.title: Claude Fable 5 Now Banned in Europe by US Government (EU-Breakdown)
item.description: Cyril Imhof: A video by Cyril Imhof discusses the claimed ban of Claude Fable 5 in Europe by the US government. The video references regulatory developments concerning Anthropic's model access across EU jurisdictions.

item.title: BREAKING: US Government BANNED Mythos and Fable
item.description: Better Stack: The US government issued an export control directive requiring Anthropic to disable Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models for all customers immediately. The source frames this as a breaking news development affecting model availability.

item.title: Google’s New AI Just Broke The AI Speed Limit: DiffusionGemma
item.description: AI Revolution: Google revealed DiffusionGemma, an open AI model that generates text using a different approach and can run up to four times faster on dedicated GPUs. The model features a 256-token canvas and self-correction capabilities.

item.title: Which Antigravity surface should I use? 2.0 vs CLI vs IDE vs SDK
item.description: Google Antigravity: Google Antigravity is an agent-first software development platform that helps developers build, orchestrate, and monitor AI agents connected to code editors, terminals, and browsers. It offers multiple interfaces including a standalone 2.0 desktop app, IDE, CLI, and SDK to support different agentic workflows.

item.title: AIventure: Vibe Coding Journey
item.description: Google for Developers: AIventure is an open source retro dungeon crawler built on Angular and Phaser.js that teaches developers about Gen AI through gameplay. It uses Gemma 4 open weights model to enable NPCs to generate code and autonomous behavior via prompting and tool calls. The game demonstrates multiple model-serving options including local inference via Transformers.js, Ollama, and LM Studio, as well as cloud deployment via Gemini API and Google Cloud.

item.title: PI AGENT FULL COURSE: Master Pi Agent in 30 Minutes
item.description: David Ondrej: David Ondrej offers a 30-minute course on Pi Agent for building real coding agents. The content covers fundamentals of using Pi Agent and includes promotions for related services including Supabase, a Skool community for AI coding, and Scale Software hiring.

item.title: Claude Fable 5 Just Changed Website Design Forever
item.description: Jack Roberts: Jack Roberts demonstrates Claude Fable 5's capabilities for website design generation compared to Opus 4.8 across three difficulty levels. The video shows Fable 5 producing competitive or superior results at lower token costs, focusing on practical applications from one-shot tests to extracting design patterns and rebuilding from blueprints.

item.title: Claude FM 🎵 music for thinking and building
item.description: Claude: Claude FM is a curated music service designed to support focus during AI development and thinking tasks. It is made and curated by musicians specifically for the Claude community.

item.title: Claude FM 🎵 music for thinking and building
item.description: Claude: Claude FM is a curated music service designed to support focused work and creative thinking for developers and builders. The music is made and curated by musicians specifically for thinking and building activities.

item.title: How to Use Higgsfield AI Like a PRO – AI Marketing Workflow
item.description: AI Master: Higgsfield Supercomputer is an AI agent that automates multi-format marketing content creation. A single prompt generates TV ads, podcast clips, vertical reels, and character-driven shorts with consistent styling and cinematic flow. The platform replaces traditional shoot-edit-repeat workflows.

item.title: Converting SQL Server code to PostgreSQL
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud demonstrates using Gemini to convert SQL Server T-SQL code to PostgreSQL, including data type translation and schema migration. The AI provides both code conversion and explanatory tutoring for developers migrating applications to the cloud.

item.title: The Rise of Generative UI for Developers (CopilotKit)
item.description: Better Stack: CopilotKit is a framework enabling developers to build agent-native applications with generative UI components. It allows agents to render real UI, share state with React or Next.js frontends, and integrate human-in-the-loop approval workflows as an alternative to bolted-on chat windows in SaaS products.

item.title: How I make my AI Agent remember everything
item.description: JavaScript Mastery: This JavaScript Mastery video covers techniques for implementing memory persistence in AI agents. The approach enables agents to retain and recall information across multiple interactions or sessions.

item.title: How to Steal Customers From Giants 600x Bigger than Your Startup | Pigment, Eléonore Crespo
item.description: EO: Eléonore Crespo, co-founder of Pigment, discusses how her AI performance management platform competes against legacy software giants by consolidating finance, supply chain, HR, and sales data. She argues that even innovative companies like Google rely on inadequate tools like spreadsheets for strategic planning, and that building a complete enterprise platform from day one while leveraging customer trust from respected companies is more effective than incremental feature releases.

item.title: The $20,000 API Key Mistake
item.description: Keith AI: Keith AI presents a case study of a $20,000 incident involving improper API key handling. The video serves as a warning about the financial risks of security mismanagement in AI API usage.

item.title: Claude Fable 5 & Apple’s NVIDIA deal
item.description: IBM Technology: IBM Technology's Mixture of Experts podcast discusses Anthropic's new Claude model and its tier routing system, Apple's partnership with NVIDIA for cloud AI at WWDC 2026, and whether AI models can detect sarcasm. Hosted by Tim Hwang with experts Christopher Hay, Kaoutar El Maghraoui, and Volkmar Uhlig.

item.title: The “Final Boss” of AI Workflows: UltraCode
item.description: Keith AI: Keith AI discusses UltraCode as a solution for AI workflows, framing it as an advanced or comprehensive approach to workflow management. The video title suggests UltraCode represents a significant capability in the AI workflow space.

item.title: Master Claude Code with Claude Fable 5 (57 mins)
item.description: Keith AI: Keith AI's 57-minute video demonstrates advanced use of Claude Code as a coding system rather than a chat interface. The video covers Desktop, CLI, and web modes, permissions, connectors, slash commands, plugins, routines, sub-agents, and dynamic workflows for building apps and automating development workflows.

item.title: Code with Claude Tokyo 2026: Opening Keynote
item.description: Claude: Anthropic's engineering and product leaders present updates at the Code with Claude 2026 opening keynote held in Tokyo, Japan. The event showcases the company's latest developments and strategic direction.

item.title: The Fable 5 Backlash Is Getting Serious
item.description: AI Revolution: Claude Fable 5 faces backlash over aggressive safety filtering that refuses harmless prompts, triggers false alarms, and limits AI-related work. Anthropic acknowledged making wrong tradeoffs with guardrails, raising questions about whether closed AI systems are becoming too controlled and whether users can trust what they are actually receiving.

item.title: The Ultimate Second Brain for AI Entrepreneurs: Fable 5 Claude with Loops
item.description: Cyril Imhof: Cyril Imhof discusses Fable 5, an Anthropic model designed for AI entrepreneurs, highlighting its features, guardrails, memory capabilities, and agentic payment integration compared to Mythos 5.

item.title: Yossi Matias on the golden age of research
item.description: Google for Developers: Yossi Matias, Vice President and Head of Google Research, discusses the "magic cycle" of research from breakthrough to real-world impact. The conversation covers AI tools for scientific discovery, speculative decoding, generative UI, and recent milestones including the Willow quantum chip.

item.title: Your first agent configuration with MCP and Looker
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud Tech explains how to build AI-powered agents using the Agent Development Kit (ADK) and Looker, integrating them with the Model Context Protocol (MCP) and MCP Toolbox for Databases. The video covers MCP setup, securing Looker API credentials, Python environment configuration, and creating agents that translate prompts into data queries.

item.title: Claude Fable 5 + YouTube = $30,000/mo
item.description: Jack Roberts: Jack Roberts demonstrates a faceless YouTube channel system combining Claude Code (Fable 5), Higsfield CLI for automated editing, and Auphonic for audio processing. The pipeline deconstructs successful channels, generates scripts via Claude, records voice-over, and uses Claude to automatically edit and stitch videos together at roughly two dollars per video.

item.title: Why Can't Anyone Answer Questions About the Business? — Garrett Galow, WorkOS
item.description: AI Engineer: Garrett Galow from WorkOS presented Studio, an internal workspace that lets non-technical users ask business questions in natural language against Snowflake, Linear, and Notion, generating reusable widgets as declarative JavaScript. The system uses preflight sequencing for schema injection, explicit distrust of model knowledge, and query validation to ensure reliability for production use.

item.title: Running 128 Coding Agents at Once
item.description: Cursor: Cursor's Sam Whitmore discusses with Baseten executives the scaling of coding agents to hundreds of instances, agent-to-agent communication and review mechanisms, and the shift from parallel task management to building integrated agent systems.

item.title: What Should An AI Device Look Like? — With Alex Himel
item.description: Alex Kantrowitz: Alex Himel, Meta's VP of Wearables, discusses why smart glasses represent the next major computing platform for AI assistants. The conversation covers AI applications in daily tasks, privacy concerns around facial recognition, on-device AI, and Meta's competitive position against OpenAI, Google, Apple, and Amazon in the AI device space.

item.title: Local AI Agents Masterclass (Gemma 4 + OpenCode + Odysseus tutorial)
item.description: Jordan Urbs: A tutorial demonstrating setup and use of local AI agents including Odysseus, an agent workspace compatible with Ollama and Gemma 4, and OpenCode, a build agent. The workflow demonstrates running frontier models for planning and local models for execution, with examples of building web applications through natural language commands.

item.title: The agent-ready web: Simplify user actions with WebMCP — Tara Agyemang, Google
item.description: AI Engineer: WebMCP is a proposed web standard introduced by Google's Chrome team that enables AI agents to interact with websites through structured, named tools instead of parsing DOM and pixel coordinates. It provides both declarative HTML attributes and imperative JavaScript APIs for registering agent-callable actions, demonstrated with a concert ticket purchase flow.

item.title: What is MCP? Learn MCP Client, MCP Server & MCP Architecture
item.description: Code With Aarohi: Model Context Protocol (MCP) is a standard that enables AI agents to connect with external tools, APIs, databases, and services through a common protocol. The video explains MCP architecture including client-server communication patterns, JSON-RPC messaging, local vs remote server configurations, and use cases with frameworks like LangGraph, CrewAI, and Claude Desktop.

item.title: How to Keep Shipping When You Walk Away from Your Desk — Zack Proser, WorkOS
item.description: AI Engineer: Zack Proser argues that human attention, not agent speed, is the bottleneck in AI-assisted development. He presents a four-layer stack of signal agents, verification gates, self-improvement loops, and holistic monitoring to enable developers to ship code while away from their desks, framing this as a solution to AI-induced burnout.

item.title: "An endless demand for compute" | Jonathan Ross, founder of Groq
item.description: Julia Turc: Groq is a hardware chip specifically designed for LLM inference. Jonathan Ross, the founder, discusses Groq's LPU architecture, its comparison to GPUs via Pareto curves and cost-per-token metrics, and a strategic agreement with NVIDIA. The interview covers technical aspects like static scheduling and mixture-of-experts models.

item.title: AI That’s Too Dangerous For You? What we learned from S.A.T.A.N
item.description: IBM Technology: IBM Technology explores how AI detects zero-day vulnerabilities faster than humans and shapes cybersecurity defense strategies. The video examines AI-driven vulnerability scanning and DevSecOps as tools for making systems safer, positioning AI as both a capability and a potential risk in security.

item.title: The AI Skill I use to prevent refactors
item.description: JavaScript Mastery: JavaScript Mastery author shares an AI-based skill or methodology they claim helps prevent software refactors. The video references a full discussion at a jobpilot resource.

item.title: NVIDIA’s Nemotron 3 Is... Awesome?
item.description: Caleb Writes Code: A technical deep dive into NVIDIA's Nemotron 3 model series, examining the architectural innovations that differentiate them from other open models. The video analyzes three key components: Hybrid Mamba, Latent Mixture of Experts, and MTP.

item.title: Opendoor’s India exit is fueling a bigger conversation about AI and outsourcing
item.description: TechCrunch AI: Opendoor's exit from India is prompting broader discussion about the relationship between AI capabilities and outsourcing practices. India's emergence as the world's largest Global Capability Center market frames this decision within larger workforce and economic trends.

item.title: Anthropic’s Dario Amodei has just one direct report
item.description: TechCrunch AI: TechCrunch AI reports that Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei has only one direct report, presented as evidence of his leadership approach or effectiveness.

item.title: Microsoft C.E.O. Satya Nadella Says ‘Everyone Is a Stakeholder’ in A.I. - The New York Times
item.description: Google News AI: Microsoft C.E.O. Satya Nadella articulated a position that "Everyone Is a Stakeholder" in artificial intelligence. This statement reflects a governance perspective on inclusive participation in AI development and deployment.

item.title: Anthropic Walks Back Policy That Could Have ‘Sabotaged’ AI Researchers Using Claude
item.description: Simon Willison: Anthropic reversed a controversial policy that would have secretly limited Claude's effectiveness on frontier LLM development tasks without user notification. The company acknowledged making the wrong tradeoff between transparency and safeguards, and committed to making such safeguards visible going forward.

item.title: Tell HN: Anthropic's Fable model is too expensive
item.description: hyhmrright: A user on Anthropic's $200 subscription plan reports that using the Fable model consumed 45% of their weekly quota in a single day for problem-solving and code review, compared to only 80% total usage per week with Opus 4.8. The post criticizes Fable's pricing efficiency relative to user quotas.

item.title: Claude Fable 5 is here!
item.description: AI Search: AI Search reviews Claude Fable 5 (also called Claude Mythos 5) and compares it to GPT 5.5. The video demonstrates the model's capabilities across diverse tasks including image understanding, 3D reconstruction, music composition, chemistry, and cancer classification, with discussion of benchmarks and specifications.

item.title: The backlash against AI, in 4 charts
item.description: 1vuio0pswjnm7: This piece examines growing skepticism and opposition to AI through four data visualizations. It captures a shift in public perception regarding AI deployment and adoption.

item.title: OpenAI mulls slashing prices as it competes with Anthropic for users: WSJ - CNBC
item.description: Google News AI: OpenAI is considering price reductions as part of competitive strategy to retain and gain users against Anthropic. The pricing move reflects intensifying competition in the large language model market between the two organizations.
